title: UNIDO National Programme Officer (NOC) Job in Abuja, Nigeria – Applications Open for 2026
summary: The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) is inviting applications for the position of National Programme Officer (NOC) based in Abuja, Nigeria . This fixed-term staff position offers an excellent opportunity for experienced development professionals to contribute to inclusive and sustainable industrial development in Nigeria. The successful candidate will work closely with government institutions, UN agencies, and development partners to design and implement programs that promote innovation, job creation, and economic growth. If you are passionate about sustainable development and want to make a direct impact on Nigeria’s industrial transformation, this role offers a meaningful career pathway within the United Nations system. About the Role The National Programme Officer will support the planning, coordination, and implementation of UNIDO’s programs in Nigeria. The role focuses on translating global development priorities into practical initiatives that advance industrial development and contribute to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will: Develop and manage national development programs and projects Coordinate with government agencies and development stakeholders Support partnerships across the UN system Monitor implementation and assess project results Contribute to policy dialogue and strategic planning Advance inclusive and sustainable industrial development objectives Why This Opportunity Matters UNIDO plays a vital role in helping countries use industry as a driver of economic growth, job creation, and environmental sustainability. This position offers the chance to contribute directly to initiatives that improve livelihoods and strengthen Nigeria’s industrial sector. Position Details Organization: UNIDO Job Title: National Programme Officer Grade: NOC Duty Station: Abuja, Nigeria Contract Type: Fixed-term staff position Diversity and Inclusion Women candidates and persons with disabilities are especially encouraged to apply. Application Deadline Applications close on: 18 May 2026 (Vienna time) How to Apply Apply here For More Job Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post UNIDO National Programme Officer (NOC) Job in Abuja, Nigeria – Applications Open for 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mozilla Festival 2026 Call for Proposals: Share Your Ideas on Technology and Society in Barcelona
summary: Applications are now open for the Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026 Call for Proposals , inviting innovators, researchers, artists, technologists, and community leaders from around the world to shape one of the most influential gatherings focused on technology and its impact on society. Organized by Mozilla Foundation , MozFest 2026 will take place in Barcelona, Spain, from October 28 to 30, 2026 . This global event provides a platform for people whose work challenges, explores, and reimagines the role of technology in our lives. Selected participants will lead engaging sessions and experiences across eight community-driven tracks curated by Mozilla’s international team of Co-Curators. About Mozilla Festival Mozilla Festival is a unique gathering where technologists, activists, educators, artists, policymakers, and community builders come together to discuss how technology can better serve humanity. The festival centers the voices of people most affected by technology and encourages collaborative exploration of issues such as digital rights, AI ethics, internet health, privacy, inclusion, and innovation. Types of Sessions Accepted Selected proposals may take the form of: Talks Open forums Hands-on labs Art experiences Multimedia installations Interactive workshops Collaborative discussions Who Should Apply Mozilla welcomes proposals from individuals and organizations whose work inspires people to: Question the impact of technology Build innovative solutions Explore emerging ideas Connect communities Ideal applicants include: Technologists and developers Researchers and academics Artists and creatives Educators Civil society leaders Policy advocates Community organizers Why Submit a Proposal? Selected participants will: Present their work to a global audience Contribute to critical conversations on technology and society Collaborate with diverse changemakers Gain visibility within the Mozilla community Help shape the future of ethical and inclusive technology Event Details Event: Mozilla Festival 2026 Location: Barcelona, Spain Dates: October 28–30, 2026 Application Deadline Submit your proposal by: 24 May 2026 How to Apply Submit your proposal here For More Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: UNFPA Malawi Internship 2026: Launch Your International Development Career with the United Nations
summary: Applications are now open for the UNFPA Malawi Internship Programme 2026 , an excellent opportunity for recent graduates and final-year students to gain hands-on experience within the United Nations system. Hosted by UNFPA Malawi , this internship offers aspiring development professionals the chance to work on high-impact initiatives focused on Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) , youth empowerment , and gender equality . Participants will receive direct mentorship from senior UN staff while contributing to meaningful projects that advance sustainable development and improve lives across Malawi. About the Internship The internship is designed to provide practical experience in international development and expose participants to the work of one of the leading UN agencies dedicated to reproductive health, gender equality, and population issues. Interns will work in a dynamic country office environment, supporting programs and operations that address critical social and health challenges. Areas of Work Interns may contribute to projects related to: Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) Youth empowerment and participation Gender equality and women s rights Programme implementation and research Monitoring, reporting, and communication Benefits of the Internship Participants will gain: Hands-on experience in a United Nations agency Mentorship from experienced UN professionals Exposure to real-world development challenges Professional networking opportunities Practical skills that can support future careers in the UN system and international development Who Should Apply This internship is ideal for: Recent university graduates Final-year students Young professionals interested in international development Candidates passionate about SRHR, youth development, and gender equality Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation, academic achievement, and interest in humanitarian and development work. Why Choose UNFPA? UNFPA is the United Nations sexual and reproductive health agency, working to ensure that every pregnancy is wanted, every childbirth is safe, and every young person s potential is fulfilled. An internship with UNFPA provides valuable experience and a strong foundation for careers in the United Nations and other global organizations. Application Deadline No specific deadline has been provided in the announcement. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. How to Apply Apply here. For more Internship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: NextGen Women Leaders Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Program for Women Aged 18–35
summary: Applications are now open for the United Leaders Global (ULG) NextGen Women Leaders Fellowship 2026 , a fully funded six-month virtual leadership program designed to equip emerging women leaders with the skills, mentorship, and global connections needed to drive meaningful social change. This prestigious fellowship will select 100 outstanding women from around the world to participate in world-class leadership training, project-based learning, and a dynamic international network of changemakers. If you are passionate about leadership, community impact, and global collaboration, this opportunity offers an exceptional platform to develop your potential and amplify your influence. About the Fellowship The NextGen Women Leaders Fellowship is a comprehensive virtual program that empowers young women to become effective and confident leaders in their communities and beyond. Participants will engage in structured leadership development, collaborative learning, and practical projects focused on social impact. The program is entirely online, making it accessible to women regardless of their location. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive: Full funding to participate in the six-month program World-class leadership and personal development training Access to a global network of emerging women leaders Project-based learning focused on real-world social impact Certificate of completion Opportunities for international collaboration and mentorship Program Highlights Duration: 6 months Format: Fully virtual Number of Fellows Selected: 100 Cost: Fully funded Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Identify as women Be between 18 and 35 years old Demonstrate a strong passion for leadership and social change Commit to full participation throughout the program Be open-minded and globally aware Possess strong English communication skills Submit two professional or academic recommendation letters Ineligibility Current United Leaders Global Fellows and Ambassadors are not eligible to apply. This opportunity is reserved for new applicants only. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 29 May 2026 How to Apply Apply here For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Presidential Leadership Scholars Program 2027: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Experienced Leaders
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Presidential Leadership Scholars Class of 2027, a fully funded leadership development program designed for bold, principled, and high-potential leaders from diverse sectors.. This highly competitive leadership development program brings together accomplished leaders from different sectors who are committed to driving positive change in their communities, organizations, and professions. The Presidential Leadership Scholars (PLS) program explores leadership lessons and experiences from the administrations of former U.S. Presidents George W. Bush, Bill Clinton, George H.W. Bush, and Lyndon B. Johnson while helping participants strengthen their leadership capacity through reflection, collaboration, and action-oriented learning. Key Details Program: Presidential Leadership Scholars Class of 2027 Location: United States Application Deadline: July 19, 2026 (11:59 PM CT) Program Duration: 6 Months Who Can Apply: Experienced leaders with typically 10+ years of professional experience Funding: Fully funded (housing, meals, and domestic travel during sessions covered) Program Format: Six in-person leadership modules + onboarding webinar Required: Personal Leadership Project (PLP) About the Presidential Leadership Scholars Program The Presidential Leadership Scholars Program is designed for bold, principled, and high-potential leaders who are ready to tackle critical societal challenges and expand their leadership impact. The six-month intensive leadership program assembles a diverse cohort of professionals from across sectors who are interested in strengthening their leadership skills while learning alongside peers from different backgrounds and perspectives. Scholars participate in immersive modules, collaborative leadership development activities, and practical leadership exercises focused on creating meaningful change. Program Benefits Successful participants will receive: Fully funded participation in the six-month leadership program Housing and meals during all program sessions Domestic travel during program modules Access to a diverse network of accomplished leaders Leadership coaching and development opportunities Exposure to leadership lessons from former U.S. presidential administrations Opportunities for collaboration and peer learning Personal and professional growth through experiential learning Who Should Apply? The Presidential Leadership Scholars Program is looking for leaders who demonstrate: Civically Minded Leadership Applicants should show a strong commitment to improving their communities, organizations, or professional sectors. Openness to Feedback Successful scholars are willing to receive, reflect on, and act upon feedback related to their leadership style and performance. Acceptance of Diverse Perspectives The program values leaders who actively engage with individuals from different backgrounds, viewpoints, and experiences. Strong Professional Experience Applicants typically have at least 10+ years of meaningful professional experience and an established leadership track record. Demonstrated Leadership PLS is designed for experienced leaders rather than individuals seeking introductory leadership training. Ability to Influence Change Applicants should already be in positions where they can drive meaningful impact and influence positive transformation. Commitment to the Program Participants must be able to fully engage in the rigorous six-month experience and attend all required sessions. Personal Leadership Project (PLP) All applicants are required to submit a Personal Leadership Project (PLP) as part of their application. The project should: Focus on creating a positive outcome for a specific group of people Include an achievable six-month goal Build on the applicant’s existing expertise, experience, or network Benefit directly from participation in the program Address a significant leadership challenge Projects may relate to current professional responsibilities or new initiatives outside an applicant’s day-to-day role. Presidential Leadership Scholars 2027 Schedule Participants are required to attend all sessions and activities. Onboarding Webinar 📅 January 6, 2027 🕑 2:00 PM – 4:00 PM (CT) 📍 Online via Zoom Program Modules Module One: January 20 – January 23, 2027 Module Two: February 24 – February 26, 2027 Module Three: March 17 – March 19, 2027 Module Four: April 21 – April 23, 2027 Module Five: May 12 – May 14, 2027 Module Six: June 23 – June 26, 2027 Application Process The application process includes: Personal and professional background information Essay responses Letters of recommendation Personal Leadership Project summary Please click “ Next ” to start your application. Important Application Timeline Application Deadline: July 19, 2026 Finalist Notifications: Mid-September 2026 Interviews: Early October 2026 Final Acceptance Decisions: December 2026 Leadership Approach The Presidential Leadership Scholars Program emphasizes inclusive leadership and meaningful collaboration. Scholars are expected to: Reflect deeply on their leadership development Build inclusive leadership habits Engage respectfully across differences Include diverse voices in decision-making Foster collaboration and participation Seek understanding even during disagreement Why You Should Apply The Presidential Leadership Scholars Program offers a unique opportunity for experienced professionals to strengthen their leadership abilities while joining a powerful network of changemakers committed to improving lives and solving pressing global and local challenges. If you are passionate about leadership, collaboration, civic engagement, and creating lasting impact, this program could be an excellent next step in your leadership journey. 🔗 Apply now and learn more through the official program website. 💡 Pro Tip: Your Personal Leadership Project is a major component of the application. Focus on presenting a realistic, impact-driven initiative that clearly demonstrates your leadership capacity, long-term vision, and ability to create measurable change. Questions? Learn more details about the program, qualifications, and application process in our frequently asked questions . Please click “ Next ” to start your application. FOR MORE INFO AND TO APPLY PLEASE VISIT https://gwbpc-pls.secure-platform.com/pls The post Presidential Leadership Scholars Program 2027: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Experienced Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Development Opportunity for Early-Career Changemakers
summary: The Annie E. Casey Foundation has officially opened applications for the second cohort of the Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship, a prestigious 21-month leadership development program designed to empower emerging leaders who are already making a difference in the lives of children, youth, families, and communities. This fully funded fellowship is specifically tailored for early-career professionals between the ages of 24 and 31 who are committed to driving equitable change in communities facing systemic challenges and limited resources. Through seminars, coaching, applied leadership practice, networking, and customized learning experiences, the program equips participants with the tools, competencies, and strategic mindset necessary to strengthen their leadership impact. About the Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship The Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship is part of the Annie E. Casey Foundation’s broader Thrive by 25™ commitment, which focuses on ensuring young people have the resources, relationships, and opportunities they need to succeed in education, employment, and life. This fellowship was intentionally created with input from young leaders across the United States and represents the Foundation’s first Results Count® leadership initiative specifically designed for individuals aged 24 to 31. The program recognizes that many young professionals are already leading transformative initiatives within their communities but often lack structured leadership development opportunities that help them expand their impact. The fellowship therefore supports individuals who are: Leading community initiatives Building strategic partnerships Influencing policy Creating social impact solutions Working across sectors to improve outcomes for vulnerable populations Participants often come from fields such as: Education Public policy Community development Youth advocacy Arts and culture Social justice initiatives Nonprofit leadership Civic engagement What Makes This Fellowship Unique? Unlike traditional leadership programs that focus mainly on theory, the Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship emphasizes real-world application. Fellows bring actual leadership challenges from their professional environments into the fellowship experience. They then work on solving these challenges using tools, frameworks, coaching, and collaborative learning acquired throughout the program. The fellowship treats leadership development as both: An internal practice — helping fellows understand themselves, strengthen self-awareness, and improve leadership behaviors. An external practice — helping fellows effectively navigate organizations, systems, partnerships, and complex social challenges. This integrated approach allows fellows to immediately apply what they learn to their professional roles and communities. Fellowship Duration and Structure The fellowship runs for 21 months and consists of multiple learning sessions, leadership engagements, and collaborative activities. The program includes: 1. Applied Leadership Work Each fellow identifies a major leadership challenge or result area connected to their work. This becomes the foundation of their fellowship journey. Participants continuously refine their approach through: Coaching Peer consultation Leadership seminars Reflection sessions Practical experimentation This ensures fellows make measurable progress while participating in the program. 2. In-Person Seminars and Virtual Learning Sessions Fellows participate in: Intensive in-person seminars Virtual leadership workshops Reflection and strategy sessions Applied learning discussions These sessions are designed to help fellows: Build leadership capacity Develop strategic thinking Strengthen communication skills Enhance collaboration Improve systems-level problem-solving The fellowship also incorporates place-based learning experiences, meaning fellows travel to different locations to engage with diverse communities and leadership contexts. 3. Coaching and Peer Learning Participants receive: Individual coaching Small-group coaching Peer feedback opportunities Collaborative learning experiences These engagements help fellows: Reflect on leadership behaviors Improve decision-making Gain practical insights Receive real-time feedback Strengthen confidence and effectiveness 4. Between-Session Engagement Learning continues even outside formal seminars. Fellows participate in: Virtual coaching sessions Leadership practice exercises Ongoing peer engagement Follow-up learning activities This structure helps maintain momentum and continuous professional growth throughout the 21-month experience. 5. Faculty Site Visits A particularly distinctive aspect of the fellowship is the structured faculty site visit component. Faculty members visit fellows within their actual organizational or work environments to: Observe leadership practice Understand organizational challenges Connect fellowship learning to real-world work Provide personalized guidance This hands-on support strengthens the practical value of the program. 6. Build Your Own Experience Each fellow develops a customized leadership development plan aligned with personal and professional goals. This component allows participants to pursue individualized learning opportunities that support their unique leadership journey and aspirations. 7. Network and Community Building The fellowship strongly emphasizes relationship-building and cross-sector collaboration. Participants become part of a growing leadership network that includes: Fellow cohort members Faculty experts Community leaders Policy professionals Change-makers across sectors These relationships often continue long after the formal fellowship concludes. Fellowship Goals The program aims to prepare fellows to become: Results-driven leaders Equity-focused professionals Effective systems changemakers Collaborative community leaders Participants will strengthen their ability to: Mobilize others around shared goals Address inequities and disparities Navigate complex systems Advance equitable outcomes Apply leadership tools effectively Create sustainable community impact Commitment to Equity and Justice A major focus of the fellowship is understanding how race, identity, systems, and structural inequities shape community outcomes. The program encourages fellows to critically examine: Systemic disparities Institutional barriers Unequal access to opportunities Community challenges Leadership responsibility in advancing equity Fellows are then supported in developing practical strategies to address these issues through their professional roles and leadership initiatives. Organizational Benefits The fellowship does not only benefit participants individually; it also strengthens the organizations and systems where fellows work. Organizations benefit because fellows: Bring new leadership tools into their workplaces Apply innovative approaches in real time Improve collaboration within teams Advance organizational goals Enhance community impact Because of the intensive nature of the fellowship, strong supervisor and organizational support is required. Selected fellows must be allowed to: Attend all seminars Participate fully during work hours Engage in leadership activities Implement fellowship learning within their organizations Financial Support and Fellowship Coverage The Annie E. Casey Foundation fully supports the core costs associated with participation. Covered expenses include: Transportation to fellowship seminars Travel-related expenses Accommodation during seminars Meals during program activities Fellowship materials Additional support may also include: Childcare stipends Accessibility support Individualized learning resources This ensures fellows can participate fully without financial barriers. Why You Should Apply The Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders to accelerate their growth while creating measurable community impact. Benefits include: Fully funded leadership development National professional network Personalized coaching Real-world leadership application Long-term career growth Cross-sector collaboration Equity-centered leadership training Practical systems-change experience For young professionals passionate about creating meaningful change, this fellowship can become a transformative career milestone. Important Deadline Applications must be submitted by: July 6, 2026, at 5 p.m. EDT APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Rising Leaders for Results Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Development Opportunity for Early-Career Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme 2026 for Young People Living With HIV, Activists and Researchers
summary: The International AIDS Society (IAS) has officially opened applications for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme, a prestigious international opportunity designed to empower young people living with HIV, youth activists, and emerging researchers in the global HIV response ecosystem. The programme is part of the 26th International AIDS Conference (AIDS 2026) and aims to strengthen youth leadership, advocacy, networking, innovation, and career development within the HIV and public health sector. Young leaders selected for the programme will gain direct exposure to global HIV experts, researchers, policymakers, advocates, and scientists while participating in a specially curated leadership and mentorship curriculum during the conference. This initiative represents a major opportunity for young professionals and advocates passionate about HIV research, public health, social justice, activism, and global health leadership. About the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme The Young Leaders Programme is an initiative offered at both the International AIDS Conference and the IAS Conference on HIV Science. It was created to support the next generation of leaders working in HIV advocacy, healthcare, research, and community mobilization. The programme seeks to provide young participants with practical exposure, professional connections, mentorship, and opportunities to actively contribute to global conversations around HIV prevention, treatment, advocacy, policy, and innovation. Selected Young Leaders will participate in exclusive sessions designed to build leadership capacity and foster collaboration among young change-makers from around the world. The programme particularly supports: Young people living with HIV Young activists Young researchers Emerging public health leaders Youth advocates working in HIV-related fields What Participants Will Gain Young Leaders selected for AIDS 2026 will benefit from an extensive learning and networking experience that goes beyond traditional conference participation. Programme Benefits Include: Meetings With Global HIV Leaders Participants will engage directly with leading scientists, researchers, policymakers, advocates, and AIDS 2026 featured speakers. Innovation Workshops Young Leaders will participate in collaborative workshops designed to help develop innovative ideas and solutions related to HIV advocacy, healthcare delivery, prevention, and research. One-on-One Career Forum Participants will receive personalized career guidance and mentorship focused on professional pathways within the HIV and public health sector. International Networking Opportunities The programme provides access to a global network of: Young activists Researchers Community advocates Public health professionals Youth leaders living with HIV Leadership Development Young Leaders will strengthen their: Advocacy skills Public engagement capacity Research communication Leadership competencies Collaborative problem-solving skills Who Can Apply? To qualify for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme, applicants must meet the following eligibility requirements: Must be aged 29 years or younger Must be registered to attend AIDS 2026 in person Must be interested or active in HIV advocacy, research, activism, or community engagement Must apply through their conference profile The programme welcomes applications from young individuals with diverse experiences and backgrounds. Important Dates Application Opening Date 4 May 2026 Application Deadline 18 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute technical challenges. How to Apply Applications for the AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme must be submitted through the applicant’s official conference profile. Application Process Register to attend AIDS 2026 in person Access your conference profile Complete the Young Leaders Programme application Submit before the deadline Official Application Link APPLY HERE Why This Opportunity Matters The HIV response movement has always been strengthened by youth leadership and community-driven innovation. The AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme provides a platform for young people to: Influence global discussions Build meaningful partnerships Advance their careers Share lived experiences Contribute to solutions that improve health outcomes worldwide For many participants, this programme can become a gateway to long-term involvement in international health leadership, research collaborations, and advocacy initiatives. Frequently Asked Questions Will Young Leaders Receive Funding? Applicants are encouraged to consult the official FAQs for information regarding funding and support. How Many Young Leaders Will Be Selected? The IAS has not publicly specified the exact number of selected participants. Where Can Applicants Ask Questions? For additional inquiries: YouthHub@iasociety.org About the International AIDS Society (IAS) The International AIDS Society is one of the world’s leading independent associations of HIV professionals. Through conferences, scientific engagement, advocacy, and community programmes, IAS works to accelerate the global HIV response and improve access to research, treatment, prevention, and care. The AIDS Conference remains one of the largest global gatherings focused on HIV science, policy, activism, and public health collaboration. Final Thoughts The AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme is more than just a conference opportunity—it is a leadership platform for the next generation of HIV advocates, researchers, and changemakers. Young people who are passionate about HIV advocacy, community impact, healthcare innovation, public health research, and global leadership should strongly consider applying for this transformative programme. With access to international experts, networking opportunities, innovation workshops, and career mentorship, participants will gain valuable experience that can shape their future impact in the global health sector. For more information on this fellowship visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here The post AIDS 2026 Young Leaders Programme 2026 for Young People Living With HIV, Activists and Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 | Fully Funded East Africa Research Opportunity for Social Scientists
summary: Researchers and scholars interested in food systems, social sciences, and African studies now have an exciting opportunity to advance their academic careers through the IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026. The programme is designed to support innovative research on the social and spatial biographies of food objects across East Africa while contributing to a growing body of interdisciplinary scholarship on food circulation, mobility, culture, and society. The Institut Français de Recherche en Afrique (IFRA-Nairobi) is offering postdoctoral fellowships ranging from three to six months for qualified researchers conducting studies connected to food circulation and food object biographies in East Africa. Selected fellows will receive fieldwork support valued at 800 Euros per month to facilitate their research activities. This fellowship is part of the broader “Food Circulations in Africa” programme, which seeks to document the social life of food objects in Africa and develop methodologies for analysing how food moves through social, economic, political, and geographical spaces. About the IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Programme The “Food Circulations in Africa” programme focuses on understanding the movement, transformation, and social significance of food objects across African societies. Rather than examining food solely as nutrition or commerce, the programme explores food as a cultural, historical, political, and social object with evolving biographies shaped by human interactions, migration, trade, identity, and globalization. The initiative aims to produce contextualized social and spatial biographies of food objects, helping scholars and policymakers better understand the interconnected dynamics of food systems in Africa. Through this postdoctoral fellowship, IFRA-Nairobi supports researchers who can contribute fresh insights into: Food circulation networks Food histories and identities Agricultural and trade systems Migration and food mobility Consumption practices Political economies of food Cultural meanings attached to food objects Social transformation through food systems Fellowship Duration and Financial Support Successful applicants will receive: A postdoctoral fellowship lasting between 3 and 6 months A fieldwork grant of 800 Euros per month Research support from IFRA-Nairobi Opportunities to contribute to regional and international academic discussions on food studies and African research The fellowship is specifically intended to support field-based research activities in East Africa. Research Areas and Academic Focus The programme welcomes interdisciplinary research projects connected to the social and spatial biographies of food objects in East Africa. Applicants are encouraged to explore themes such as: 1. Food Mobility and Circulation Research examining how food products move across local, national, and regional boundaries. 2. Food and Identity Studies exploring cultural identity, heritage, and community relationships linked to food. 3. Political and Economic Dimensions of Food Projects analysing food governance, trade systems, food security, and agricultural economies. 4. Historical and Anthropological Perspectives Research documenting historical food pathways, traditions, and evolving food practices. 5. “Follow the Thing” Methodologies Projects inspired by methodologies that trace the movement and transformation of objects through social systems. Eligible Countries and Research Regions The fellowship targets researchers conducting studies in East Africa, specifically in: Kenya Uganda Tanzania Rwanda Burundi Eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) Applicants must currently reside in a country within the East African region. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the IFRA-Nairobi Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic Qualifications Applicants must: Have defended a PhD thesis in the social sciences or humanities after January 1, 2020 Research Areas Candidates should conduct research related to East Africa in one or more of the following disciplines: Geography Anthropology Political Science History Related social science and humanities fields Residency Requirement Applicants must reside in an East African country. Age Requirement Applicants must be under 45 years old. Required Application Documents Applicants are expected to prepare and submit the following materials: 1. Cover Letter The cover letter should: Be approximately 2 pages long Include a 15–20 line summary of the research project Explain the applicant’s profile and research interests Highlight experience in food studies or related research fields Include contact information for Master’s or PhD supervisors Mention any additional institutional support or funding received The document must be submitted in PDF format. 2. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV) The CV should include: Academic background Research experience Relevant publications Professional achievements Conferences and academic contributions The CV must be submitted in PDF format. 3. Research Proposal The proposal should be a maximum of 3 pages and must contain: Research context Research questions Methodology Bibliography Fieldwork location details Research timeline and planning Applicants are required to submit the proposal in both PDF and Word formats. Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 26 June 2026 Late submissions may not be considered. How to Apply Applications should be submitted via email to: 📧 info@ifra-nairobi.net Additional Information: IFRA-Nairobi Fellowship Information Why This Fellowship Matters The IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Fellowship represents an important opportunity for emerging scholars interested in food systems, African studies, and interdisciplinary social science research. The programme provides: Financial support for fieldwork Academic visibility Regional research engagement Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration Access to networks in African food studies and humanities research As food systems continue to shape debates around sustainability, culture, mobility, and economic development, this fellowship supports research that contributes to understanding Africa’s rapidly changing food landscapes. Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here The post IFRA-Nairobi Food Circulations in Africa Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 | Fully Funded East Africa Research Opportunity for Social Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide
summary: Global Artivism has announced multiple remote global opportunities for 2026, offering paid roles for cultural organizers, activists, researchers, campaign coordinators, and operational professionals across different world regions. These opportunities are designed for individuals deeply committed to social justice, cultural transformation, ecological integrity, and collective liberation. Led by Global Majority communities, Global Artivism is building a worldwide movement that harnesses the power of arts and culture to drive systemic change. The organization is seeking talented professionals and community-rooted changemakers to support major initiatives such as Global Artivism Month, regional ecosystem mapping, movement coordination, and global cultural activism campaigns. The available opportunities include: Admin Coordinator Global Artivism Month Specialist Regional Fellow (Africa) Regional Fellow (Americas Caribbean) Regional Fellow (Asia) Regional Fellow (Europe) Regional Fellow (Middle East / West Asia) Regional Fellow (Oceania / Pacific) These positions are fully remote and open globally or regionally depending on the role. Most opportunities prioritize candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities. About Global Artivism Global Artivism is a Global Majority-led movement that uses arts and culture as powerful tools for justice, liberation, and systems change. The movement believes that art is not separate from activism but is instead an active force that can: Shift narratives Mobilize communities Influence public consciousness Build solidarity Reshape systems of power Global Artivism connects artists, activists, cultural workers, organizers, and movement builders across multiple regions of the world. The movement is governed by a Global Council representing diverse regions and communities globally. Core Principles of Global Artivism The organization’s work is guided by several foundational principles: Global Majority Leadership and South–South Solidarity Distributed Power and Fluid Leadership Artists at the Center, Culture as Strategy Intersectional and Community-Centered Organizing Trust Before Scale Emergence as Method Transparency and Accountability These principles shape every aspect of the organization’s programming, campaigns, collaborations, and operational structure. 1. Admin Coordinator Opportunity 2026 Global Artivism is hiring an experienced administrative professional to support the Movement Secretariat through coordination, scheduling, logistics, and communication management. Contract Details Compensation: USD $1,500–$2,500/month Location: Remote (Global) Priority: Candidates from the Global South Duration: June 1, 2026 – May 31, 2027 Type: Independent Contractor Key Responsibilities The Admin Coordinator will: Coordinate meetings across multiple time zones Support virtual and in-person event logistics Draft correspondence and manage documentation Organize schedules and action items Maintain systems across Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, and project management tools Coordinate travel arrangements and meeting logistics Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Strong organizational and administrative experience Ability to support distributed international teams Excellent communication skills in English Experience with digital collaboration platforms High discretion and professionalism Alignment with Global Artivism values Community organizing and grassroots movement support experience are valued equally alongside formal administrative employment. Learn More Here 2. Global Artivism Month Specialist 2026 This role focuses on leading the delivery of Global Artivism Month, a major flagship programme planned for October/November 2026. Contract Details Compensation: USD $3,500–$5,000/month Location: Remote (Global) Duration: June 1 – December 31, 2026 Type: Independent Contractor Key Responsibilities The specialist will: Coordinate Global Artivism Month campaign delivery Build campaign toolkits and partner resources Manage timelines, budgets, and logistics Mobilize regional partners and community networks Facilitate steering committee engagement Lead post-campaign analysis and reporting Desired Experience Global Artivism is seeking candidates with: Strong programme management skills Campaign coordination experience Experience in social justice, arts, or cultural activism Community mobilization expertise Multilingual ability (preferred) Experience coordinating across global teams The organization strongly values applicants with Global South and movement-building experience. Learn More Here 3. Regional Fellowship Opportunities 2026 Global Artivism is also recruiting six Regional Fellows to strengthen regional Artivist ecosystems and coordinate community activation initiatives. Available Regions Regional Fellow positions are available for: Africa Americas Caribbean Asia Europe Middle East / West Asia Oceania / Pacific Fellowship Details Compensation: USD $900–$1,500/month Duration: June 1 – December 31, 2026 Type: Independent Contractor Work Format: Fully Remote Purpose of the Fellowship Regional Fellows will help: Build regional Artivist ecosystems Conduct cultural activism mapping and research Organize regional gatherings and exchanges Support Global Artivism Month planning Build solidarity across regional movements Main Responsibilities Regional Fellows will: Map artists, activists, organizations, and cultural movements Produce ecosystem reports and research outputs Convene regional gatherings and peer exchanges Support campaign coordination and storytelling Participate in cross-regional learning exchanges Qualifications Applicants should have: Strong community grounding in their region Experience in activism, organizing, or cultural work Research and facilitation skills Ability to coordinate networks and gatherings Strong written English communication Familiarity with social justice and movement ecosystems Global Artivism particularly values lived experience, grassroots work, and community leadership. Compensation Across Opportunities The programme offers competitive contractor fees depending on the role: Role Compensation Admin Coordinator USD $1,500–$2,500/month Global Artivism Month Specialist USD $3,500–$5,000/month Regional Fellows USD $900–$1,500/month All positions are independent contractor engagements. Contractors are responsible for their own taxes, registrations, and compliance requirements within their countries. What Global Artivism Offers Selected participants will receive: Access to a global network of Artivists and changemakers Professional development opportunities Participation in international movement-building spaces Flexible remote work arrangements Cross-regional collaboration opportunities Exposure to global arts and justice ecosystems Regional Fellows will additionally join a cohort of fellows from six global regions. How to Apply Applicants must send their applications via email to: team@globalartivism.ca Application Deadline May 17, 2026 at midnight SAST (South Africa Standard Time). Required Documents Applicants must submit: A CV (maximum two pages) A short written response (300–400 words) addressing role-specific questions One reference with contact information Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by May 25, 2026 and invited to interview rounds. Why These Opportunities Matter Global Artivism’s 2026 opportunities are more than jobs or fellowships. They are invitations to participate in a growing global movement that recognizes culture and creativity as central tools for justice and transformation. These opportunities are particularly valuable for: Artists working in activism spaces Community organizers Cultural strategists Social justice advocates Movement builders Researchers focused on arts and social change The organization’s emphasis on Global Majority leadership and community-rooted experience makes these opportunities especially inclusive for underrepresented voices and grassroots leaders globally. Discover more Job , Fellowship , and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. The post Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators
summary: The Foresight Institute has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious Foresight Fellowship 2027 , a year-long international fellowship designed to support exceptional early-career scientists, engineers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators working on transformative technologies. The fellowship provides selected participants with access to world-class mentorship, career development support, global research networks, technical workshops, funding connections, and exclusive opportunities to collaborate with some of the leading thinkers shaping the future of science and technology. Focused on accelerating breakthrough innovation, the Foresight Fellowship is ideal for ambitious individuals seeking to make meaningful contributions in areas such as artificial intelligence, neurotechnology, biotechnology, nanotechnology, space innovation, and existential risk reduction. About the Foresight Fellowship The Foresight Fellowship is a globally recognized program aimed at empowering visionary early-career innovators by connecting them with the networks, mentorship, and resources necessary to advance frontier technologies. The program is built on the belief that talented individuals can make transformative contributions when given early access to: Senior scientific mentorship Collaborative research communities Funding networks Knowledge exchange platforms High-impact scientific ecosystems The fellowship supports participants in developing both their technical expertise and long-term career trajectories. What the Fellowship Offers Selected Fellows receive extensive professional and research support throughout the year-long program. Key Benefits of the Fellowship 1. Access to Senior Scientists and Mentors Fellows are introduced to: Leading researchers Industry experts Experienced mentors Influential funders Frontier technology innovators These connections help Fellows strengthen their work and accelerate career growth. 2. Paid Participation in Major Scientific Events The fellowship includes: Attendance at technical workshops Participation in the Vision Weekend conference Fully funded travel support These events gather influential leaders working at the cutting edge of science and technology. 3. Membership in Specialized Seminar Groups Fellows gain access to: Research seminars Knowledge-sharing communities Opportunities to present their work Collaborative learning environments This allows participants to receive feedback and engage with experts in their field. 4. Priority Access to AI Research Hubs Fellows receive priority access to Foresight Institute AI Nodes located in: San Francisco Berlin These hubs support researchers and innovators working on AI for science and AI safety. 5. Career Counseling and Long-Term Community Support The fellowship also offers: Quarterly career counseling calls Guidance from industry leaders Long-term alumni network access Continued collaboration opportunities Participants become part of the broader Foresight Fellowship Alumni community after completing the program. Focus Areas of the Fellowship The fellowship supports projects and innovators working across several frontier science and technology domains. 1. Secure AI Areas include: Computer security AI alignment Secure multiagent systems AI safety research 2. Longevity Biotechnology Focus areas include: Research automation Healthcare innovation Reversible biostasis Longevity science 3. Nanotechnology Applicants may work on: Molecular 3D printing Molecular simulation Advanced nanomaterials Nanotechnology modeling systems 4. Neurotechnology Projects may involve: Brain-computer interfaces Whole brain emulation Neuro-inspired AI systems Cognitive enhancement technologies 5. Existential Hope The fellowship also supports work in: Technological alignment Strategic foresight Existential opportunity Future worldbuilding Global resilience 6. Space Innovation The fellowship welcomes innovators developing technologies and systems related to space exploration and sustainability. Who the Fellowship Is Looking For The Foresight Fellowship seeks highly motivated individuals with strong technical capabilities and ambitious visions for the future. Ideal Candidates Should Demonstrate 1. Technical Excellence Applicants should have: Strong academic or professional achievements Research publications Innovative projects Laboratory experience Proven technical expertise 2. Ambitious Vision The fellowship prioritizes “moonshot” thinkers who: Challenge conventional thinking Pursue transformative ideas Aim to solve major global problems Develop futuristic solutions 3. Commitment to Growth Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate: Intellectual curiosity Continuous learning Passion for innovation Desire to collaborate and expand their capabilities 4. Potential for Societal Impact The program strongly values projects capable of generating meaningful positive impact on society and humanity’s future. Why This Fellowship Matters The Foresight Fellowship stands out because it combines: Mentorship Scientific collaboration Career development Networking Frontier research exposure Unlike traditional fellowships, the program offers a highly flexible environment with no minimum work requirement, focusing instead on empowering exceptional individuals to maximize their potential. The fellowship creates opportunities for participants to accelerate groundbreaking work capable of shaping the future of science and technology globally. Networking and Community Opportunities Participants become part of an international community of: Scientists Engineers Entrepreneurs AI researchers Biotech innovators Futurists Technology builders This collaborative ecosystem helps Fellows exchange ideas, develop partnerships, and gain visibility for their work. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply for the 2027 cohort through the official Foresight Fellowship application portal. Official Website: Foresight Fellowship Official Website Applicants are encouraged to prepare detailed information showcasing: Their technical work Research achievements Future vision Potential societal impact Innovative projects Apply HERE Final Thoughts The Foresight Fellowship 2027 presents an exceptional opportunity for early-career innovators seeking to work at the forefront of transformative science and technology. By offering mentorship, research exposure, global networking opportunities, funded event participation, and long-term career support, the fellowship empowers visionary individuals to accelerate breakthrough innovations that could shape humanity’s future. Scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and researchers with bold ideas and strong technical foundations should strongly consider applying for this prestigious global fellowship. For more information about the opportunity, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
